text.title: Helm Values Diff Reviewer Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Helm Values Diff Reviewer` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The review MUST compare rendered manifests (`helm template`) across the bump, not just `Chart.yaml` version + `values.yaml` text diff.
rule.rationale: Values-text diff hides chart-internal default changes; rendered-manifest diff is the only deterministic surface.

rule.statement: Each rendered-diff hunk MUST be tagged `low | medium | high | critical` with rationale, no untagged hunks allowed.
rule.rationale: Bulk-approval of un-classified diffs is the #1 silent-regression cause; per-hunk class forces explicit acceptance.

rule.statement: Each risk tier MUST have a single named human approver in the matrix (no group handles, no channel mentions). `high|critical` requires platform-lead sign-off in addition to the release-owner.
rule.rationale: Group-handle approvers cause sign-off to evaporate; named approval is auditable.

rule.statement: Each `high|critical` change MUST list a one-line rollback step (helm rollback target revision + watch command) in the artefact.
rule.rationale: If rollback is undocumented at review time, MTTR balloons during the inevitable regression.

rule.statement: Each diff hunk MUST carry an evidence link (upstream changelog entry, CVE, ticket, ADR). Hunks without evidence are demoted to `needs-investigation`.
rule.rationale: Hunks without source links lead reviewers to skim; evidence-anchor forces the reviewer to read the actual change.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
